_doc_: Shared MQTT and mail credentials served by credentials.php to both
the controller (running on each customer's IXHUB) and the web UI.
DEPLOYMENT:
1. Copy this file, fill in the real values.
2. Rename to match the host that will serve credentials.php — for
production that's `rbrheating.com.txt`.
3. Upload to ONE LEVEL ABOVE the site root (e.g. /home/rbrheating/),
not into /home/rbrheating/rbrheating.com/. credentials.php reads
`../<HTTP_HOST>.txt` so a request to https://rbrheating.com/
credentials.php returns the contents of /home/rbrheating/
rbrheating.com.txt verbatim.
4. Make sure the file is NOT inside the document root — Apache
would otherwise serve it directly without auth.
FIELDS:
broker         — MQTT broker hostname (no scheme)
port           — websocket port for the UI (controller hardcodes 1883)
username       — shared MQTT username
password       — shared MQTT password
mail_server    — SMTP server for verification / password-reset emails
mail_login     — SMTP login (usually the same as mail_from)
mail_password  — SMTP password
mail_from      — From: header on outgoing mail
Per-system fields are NOT here — the controller's MAC is read from the
host's network interface, and the UI prompts the user for it once and
caches it in localStorage.
